d100a3fa-3b75-4dd2-bb47-3cb053a482b7.text:
Time to Take Emoji Seriously: They Vastly Improve Casual Conversational Models

Pieter Delobelle and Bettina Berendt KU Leuven, Department of Computer Science Celestijnenlaan 200A box 2402, 3000 Leuven, Belgium { pieter.delobelle, bettina.berendt } @cs.kuleuven.be

Abstract

Graphical emoji are ubiquitous in modern-day online conversations.

d0012a67-57e8-4b4a-8767-2033783b68cc.label: text_chunk
d0012a67-57e8-4b4a-8767-2033783b68cc.text:
So is a single thumbs-up emoji able to signify an agreement, without any words. We argue that the current state-of-the-art systems are ill-equipped to correctly interpret these emoji, especially in a conversational context. However, in a casual context, the benefits might be high: a better understanding of users’ utterances and more natural, emoji-rich responses.

With this in mind, we modify BERT to fully support emoji, both from the Unicode Standard and custom emoji.

d1a7ccda-6410-44cb-9be0-9f8fcb2a78a6.label: text_chunk
d1a7ccda-6410-44cb-9be0-9f8fcb2a78a6.text:
This modified BERT is then trained on a corpus of question-answer (QA) tuples with a high number of emoji, where we’re able to increase the 1-of-100 accuracy from 12.7% for the current state-of-the-art to 17.8% for our model with emoji support.

1 Introduction

The prevalent use of emoji—and their text-based precursors—is mostly unaddressed in current natural language processing (NLP) tasks.

3a9ff709-2826-410f-a29b-b4d6820da9eb.label: text_chunk
3a9ff709-2826-410f-a29b-b4d6820da9eb.text:
The support of the Unicode Stan- dard [31] for emoji characters in 2010 ushered in a wide-spread, international adoption of these graphical elements in casual contexts. Interpreting the meaning of these char- acters has been challenging however, since they take on multiple semantic roles [24].

Whether or not emoji are used depends on the context of a text or conversation, with more formal settings generally being less tolerating.

971b76f8-6e53-4154-8393-032c27aad6a4.label: text_chunk
971b76f8-6e53-4154-8393-032c27aad6a4.text:
So is the popular aligned corpus Europarl [16] naturally devoid of emoji. Technical limitations, like no Unicode support, also limit its use. This in turn affects commonly used corpora, tokenizers, and pre-trained networks.Take for example the Ubuntu Dialog Corpus by Lowe et al. [20], a commonly used corpus for multi-turn systems.

 This dataset was collected from an Internet Relay Chat (IRC) room casually discussing the operating system Ubuntu.

d6633a80-6062-4a7c-9f64-0e975acd0454.label: text_chunk
d6633a80-6062-4a7c-9f64-0e975acd0454.text:
IRC nodes usually

support the ASCII text encoding, so there’s no support for graphical emoji.

 However, in the 7,189,051 utterances, there are only 9946 happy emoticons (i.e.

a8436cb2-45b4-4aff-b405-13ed4c1e4f90.label: text_chunk
a8436cb2-45b4-4aff-b405-13ed4c1e4f90.text: :-) and the cruelly denosed :) version) and 2125 sad emoticons.Word embeddings are also handling emoji poorly: Word2vec [22] with the com- monly used pre-trained Google News vectors doesn’t support the graphical emoji at all and vectors for textual emoticons are inconsistent.

eac4a978-fa77-4755-a9d5-14062542c2b2.label: text_chunk
eac4a978-fa77-4755-a9d5-14062542c2b2.text: As another example with contextu- alized word embeddings, there are also no emoji or textual emoticons in the vocabulary list of BERT [6] by default and support for emoji is only recently added to the tokenizer. The same is true for GPT-2 [26].

7b1ce4bd-40a1-45f6-a2e9-57a31c1c8c89.label: text_chunk
7b1ce4bd-40a1-45f6-a2e9-57a31c1c8c89.text: As all downstream systems, ranging from multilingual r´esum´e parsing to fallacy detection [5], rely on the completeness of these embeddings, this lack of emoji support can affect the performance of some of these systems.Another challenge is that emoji usage isn’t static. Think of shifting conventions, different cultures, and newly added emoji to the Unicode list. Several applications also use their own custom emoji, like chat application Slack and streaming service Twitch.

0eee629c-20fd-4001-afe2-7a7cb4f1b2c6.label: text_chunk
0eee629c-20fd-4001-afe2-7a7cb4f1b2c6.text: This becomes an issue for methods that leverage the Unicode description [7] or that rely on manual annotations [32].Our contribution with this paper is two-fold: firstly, we argue that the current use— or rather non-existing use—of emoji in the tokenizing, training, and the datasets them- selves is insufficient. Secondly, we attempt to quantify the significance of incorporating emoji-based features by presenting a fine-tuned model.

8467595e-1706-4f8f-8516-da200e92939b.label: text_chunk
8467595e-1706-4f8f-8516-da200e92939b.text: We then compare this model to a baseline, but without special attention to emoji.Section 2 will start with an overview of work on emoji representations, emoji-based models and analysis of emoji usage. A brief introduction in conversational systems will also be given. Section 3 will then look into popular datasets with and without emoji and then introduce the dataset we used.Our model will then be discussed in Section 4, including the tokenization in Sub- section 4.

a1e9c599-3b27-4b71-9230-6952fa74f4eb.label: text_chunk
a1e9c599-3b27-4b71-9230-6952fa74f4eb.text:
1, training setup in Subsection 4.2 and evaluation in Subsection 4.3. This brings us to the results of our experiment, which is discussed in Section 5 and finally our conclusion and future work are presented in Section 6.

2 Related work

Inspired by the work on word representations, Eisner et al. [7] presented Emoji2vec.

f4cb72f7-5658-4492-ad3c-ab5dbaccc90c.label: text_chunk
f4cb72f7-5658-4492-ad3c-ab5dbaccc90c.text:
This system generates a vector representation that’s even compatible with the Word2vec representations, so they can be used together. This compatibility makes it easy to quickly incorporate Emoji2vec in existing systems that use Word2vec.The main drawback is that the emoji representations are trained on the Unicode descriptions.

 As a consequence, the representations only capture a limited meaning and do not account for shifting or incorrect use of emoji in the real world.

9abbdae9-8341-4c4b-b372-73cc05984aa2.label: text_chunk
9abbdae9-8341-4c4b-b372-73cc05984aa2.text:
For example, a peach emoji could be considered a double entendre, due to the resemblance to a woman’s posterior. This is of course mentioned nowhere in the Unicode description. Which shows that the meaning of an emoji is how users interpret it, so also accidental incorrect use can cause issues [23].In spirit, Felbo et al.

 [8] is similar to our work.

a00ee7c6-4faf-4100-9095-1abc506875ab.label: text_chunk
a00ee7c6-4faf-4100-9095-1abc506875ab.text:
Their system, DeepMoji, illustrates the importance of emoji for sentiment, emotion, and sarcasm classification. For these

20c6e76c-f60e-4748-8089-81e1f20206ef.text: tasks, they used a dataset of 1246 million tweets containing at least one emoji. How- ever, the authors use the emoji in those tweets not for the DeepMoji model input, but as an target label. With a slightly better agreement score than humans on the sentiment task, this supports our hypothesis that emoji carry the overall meaning of an utterance.Barbieri et al. [2] focus on a predicting one emoji based on the textual content.

ae170268-2232-4fb4-9881-c95a9a5e5bc4.label: text_chunk
ae170268-2232-4fb4-9881-c95a9a5e5bc4.text: Interestingly, they looked into both English and Spanish tweets and compared a range of systems for a shared task at SemEval 2018: Multilingual Emoji Prediction. This shared task shows that emoji are getting more attention, but how their task is set up also highlights the current lack of high quality datasets with emoji.The same shared task was tackled by [4] and a year later by Huang et al.

93cb9e73-5da2-4874-8203-44efda104b7d.label: text_chunk
93cb9e73-5da2-4874-8203-44efda104b7d.text: [13], which made use of a pre-processor and tokenizer from Baziotis et al. [3]. This tokenizer replaces some emoji and emoticons by tokens related to their meaning. So is \o/ replaced with <happy> . Naturally, this approach suffers from the same issues as described before.

95b87044-3fb8-4957-a55f-d4a4359898b2.label: text_chunk
95b87044-3fb8-4957-a55f-d4a4359898b2.text:
And even though it’s really useful to have some basic, out-of-the-box support for emoticons thanks to this work, we think that this strategy is too reducing to capture subtle nuances.

An analysis on the use of emoji on a global scale is done by Ljubeˇsi´c and Fiˇser [19]. For this, the authors used geo-tagged tweets, which also allowed them to correlate the popularity of certain emoji with development indicators.

24865525-351a-489d-afdf-009d80637c24.label: text_chunk
24865525-351a-489d-afdf-009d80637c24.text:
This shows that the infor- mation encoded by emoji—and of course the accompanying tweet—is not limited to sentiment or emotion. Also Ai et al. [1] analyze the uses of emoji on social networks.

 Their approach consists of finding information networks between emoji and English words with LINE [30].An interesting aspect of emoji usage is analyzed by Robertson et al. [28].

9dc34e39-c7e2-454e-8576-4775de3b3101.label: text_chunk
9dc34e39-c7e2-454e-8576-4775de3b3101.text: In this work, the correlation between the use of Fitzpatrick skin tone [9] modifiers and the perceived skin tone of the user. This research shows that users are inclined to use representing emoji for themselves. Robertson et al. [28] reported that no negative sen- timent was associated with specific skin tone modifiers.

70f2389e-220a-45bc-b53a-12a19fd18b08.label: text_chunk
70f2389e-220a-45bc-b53a-12a19fd18b08.text:
Conversational AI systems The research on conversational AI has been focussing on various aspects, including building high-quality datasets [11, 17, 18, 20, 27, 36], adding customizable personalities [21, 36, 37] or conjoining the efforts with regard to different datasets, models and evaluation practices [11]. With these combined efforts,

58a23a99-53c4-488a-a344-2712a33e4d33.text: Figure 1: Illustration of the use of BERT for a response selection task.businesses and the general public quickly began developing ambitious use-cases, like customer support agents on social networks.The proposed models in this field are diverse and largely depending on how the problem is formulated. When considering free-form responses, generative models like GPT [25], GPT-2 [26] or seq2seq [29] are appropriate.

7a8db117-c437-4090-8383-4488c3689ec8.label: text_chunk

When the conversational task is modeled as a response selection task to pick the correct response out of N candi- dates [10, 11, 35], this can be a language model like BERT [6] with a dedicated head.

3 Emoji-rich datasets are hard to ﬁnd

Emoji are commonly used in casual settings, like on social media or in casual conversa- tions.

775f4e3a-e8a7-4d72-9488-3f4f97cc419f.label: text_chunk
775f4e3a-e8a7-4d72-9488-3f4f97cc419f.text: In conversations—as opposed to relatively context-free social media posts—an emoji alone can be an utterance by itself. And with a direct impact for some applica- tions, like customer support, we focus on conversational datasets. We hope the conver- sational community has the most direct benefit from these emoji-enabled models.

a44f27e1-e303-4fd9-9ab2-9512df889b05.label: text_chunk
a44f27e1-e303-4fd9-9ab2-9512df889b05.text: Of course, the conclusions we’ll draw don’t have to be limited to this field.Table 1 gives an overview of frequently used and interesting conversational datasets. The lacuna of emoji-rich reference datasets was already mentioned in Section 1 and is in our opinion one of the factors that emoji remain fairly underutilized.For our models, we’ll use a customer support dataset with a relatively high usage of emoji.

306c2767-1563-4b09-9a68-164e0444db69.label: text_chunk
306c2767-1563-4b09-9a68-164e0444db69.text: The dataset contains 2000 tuples collected by Hori and Hori [12] that are sourced from Twitter. They provide conversations, which consist of at least one ques- tion and one free-form answer. Some conversations are longer, in this case we ignored the previous context and only looked at the last tuple. This dataset illustrates that even when contacting companies, Twitter users keep using emoji relatively often, 8.75% of all utterances.

aaba4771-076a-4f27-bead-3dcabc6d9199.label: text_chunk
aaba4771-076a-4f27-bead-3dcabc6d9199.text:
The tweets were filtered on hyper links and personal identifiers, but Unicode emoji characters were preserved. As emoji are frequently used on Twitter, this resulted in a dataset with 170 of the 2000 tuples containing at least one emoji character.

4 Fine-tuning BERT with emoji support

We continue training of a multilingual BERT model [6] with new tokens for emoji and fine-tune this model and a baseline on the dataset discussed in Section 4.

bd4e7fb8-b98d-4be3-b6b9-996c8b24c7e6.label: text_chunk
bd4e7fb8-b98d-4be3-b6b9-996c8b24c7e6.text:
This approach
4
Figure 2: Illustration the tokenization process for BERT. The illustrated input is an actual sample from the test set. Notice the emoji is replaced by a descriptive token.is explained in Subsection 4.1 and the training itself is discussed in Subsection 4.2.

 At last, the evaluation is then discussed in Subsection 4.3.

c5ac5aa4-88cb-4efb-aae9-ee2cb1d7a1f2.label: text_chunk
c5ac5aa4-88cb-4efb-aae9-ee2cb1d7a1f2.text:
4.1 Tokenizing emoji

We add new tokens to the BERT tokenizer for 2740 emoji from the Unicode Full Emoji List [31, v12.0], as well as some aliases (in the form of :happy: as is a common notation for emoji). In total, 3627 emoji tokens are added to the vocabulary.We converted all UTF-8 encoded emoji to a textual alias for two reasons.

747c5a6e-70c9-4dbd-a7f1-6138c9da5e3d.label: text_chunk
747c5a6e-70c9-4dbd-a7f1-6138c9da5e3d.text:
First, this mitigates potential issues with text encodings that could drop the emoji.

 Second, this is also a common notation format for custom emoji, so we have one uniform token format. Aside from this attention to emoji, we use WordPiece embeddings [34] in the same manner as Devlin et al. [6].

369697bb-afaf-42e6-9de1-54b40154a592.label: text_chunk
369697bb-afaf-42e6-9de1-54b40154a592.text:
4.2 Training and ﬁne-tuning

We start from 12-headed multilingual BERT ( bert-base-multilingual-cased ), which has 110M parameters. For the model with emoji support, the number of tokens is increased, so new vectors are appended at the end of the embeddings matrix. We then continue training on the language modeling task. We use the default configuration as is also used by Devlin et al.

254a5ff9-ab76-42be-9cdb-ca4deb1996f0.label: text_chunk
254a5ff9-ab76-42be-9cdb-ca4deb1996f0.text:
[6] where randomly selected tokens are replaced by:

• a mask token : 80% chance,

• another random word : 10% chance,

• the original word : 10% chance.

5
This model is trained for 100 epochs with the Adam [15] optimizer. The learning rate is set to the commonly used lr = 5 · 10 − 5 and = 10 − 8 .

34bb853c-cec3-4085-9357-ad80207b74a1.label: text_chunk
34bb853c-cec3-4085-9357-ad80207b74a1.text:
No hyper-parameter tuning was done, as the results are acceptable on their own and are sufficient to allow conclusions for this paper. The loss is cross entropy [14].

We then fine-tune both models, with and without emoji tokenization, on the sen- tence prediction task with a training set of 70%. We again use the Adam optimizer with the same settings and with binary cross entropy. In this case, the training was limited to 10 epochs.

cb54f8df-a10e-49bf-b839-885d6841271f.label: text_chunk
cb54f8df-a10e-49bf-b839-885d6841271f.text:
To mitigate the need for weighting and other class imbalance issues, we trained with pairs of positive and negative candidates. This is in contract to the evaluation, where 99 negative candidates are used.

 However, since each candi- date is considered on its own merit during evaluation, this discrepancy won’t affect the performance.For the formulation of the fine-tuning task, we use the same approach as Devlin et al. [6].

64936490-18f4-454a-8646-2c74146691c4.label: text_chunk
64936490-18f4-454a-8646-2c74146691c4.text:
The first input sentence is joined with the second sentence, separated by a special [SEP] token, as can be seen in Figure 2. The model, with a specialized head for next sentence prediction , then outputs a correlation score.

4.3 evaluation metrics

Finally, our model is compared against the pre-trained version of BERT without special emoji tokens. We evaluate both this baseline and our model as a response selection task.

c8e8e786-abc2-4767-af5f-5bdb2c600563.label: text_chunk
c8e8e786-abc2-4767-af5f-5bdb2c600563.text:
In this case, the system has to select the most appropriate response out N = 100 candidates. This is a more restricted problem, where the 1-of-100 accuracy [11] is a popular evaluation metric.Note that 1-in-100 accuracy gives a summary of the model performance for a par- ticular dataset.

 Since not all 99 negative responses are necessarily bad choices, the resulting score is in part dependent on the prior distribution of a dataset.

dc03deb9-e355-4661-badc-9e2ee734cd5d.label: text_chunk
dc03deb9-e355-4661-badc-9e2ee734cd5d.text:
For example, Henderson et al. [11] compares models for three datasets, where the best performing model has a score of 30.6 for OpenSubtitles [18] and 84.2 for AmazonQA [33].Aside from the 1-of-100 accuracy, we also present the mean rank of the correct response.

 Since the Twitter dataset is focussed on customer service, the correct re- sponse is sometimes similar to others.

6c509edb-7a40-4d7e-b1ed-a2f9d89b7d57.label: text_chunk
6c509edb-7a40-4d7e-b1ed-a2f9d89b7d57.text: The mean rank, also out of N = 100 , can differentiate whether or not the model is still selecting good responses. For each input sentence, a rank of 1 means the positive response is ranked highest and is thus correctly selected and a rank of N signifies the positive response was—incorrectly—considered the worst-matching candidate.

44130c7c-a028-4fc4-9493-746c1709296f.text:
5 Emoji provide additional context to response selec- tion models

After training of the language model with additional tokens for all Unicode emoji, we achieved a final perplexity of 2.0281. For comparison, the BERT model with 16 heads achieved a perplexity of 3.23 [6], but this is on a general dataset.For the sentence prediction task, Table 2 shows the results of the baseline and our model with additional emoji tokens.

dc80c5e9-1bcd-48ed-b018-0117ed899a30.label: text_chunk
dc80c5e9-1bcd-48ed-b018-0117ed899a30.text: The 1-out-of-100 accuracy measures how often the true candidate was cor- rectly selected and the mean rank gives an indication of how the model performs if it fails to correctly select the positive candidate.The baseline correctly picks 12.7% of all candidate responses, out of 100. Given that the dataset is focussed on support questions and multiple responses are likely to be relevant, this baseline already performs admirable.

dca7eee4-5ada-4959-afd5-a458d4287b5c.label: text_chunk
dca7eee4-5ada-4959-afd5-a458d4287b5c.text: For reference, a BERT model on the OpenSubtitles dataset [18] achieves a 1-of-100 accuracy between 12.2% and 17.5%, depending on the model size [11].Our model improves on this baseline with a 1-of-100 accuracy of 17.8%. The mean rank remains almost the same.

5798f7fb-de67-4941-9ee7-53391d99bfdb.label: text_chunk
5798f7fb-de67-4941-9ee7-53391d99bfdb.text:
This indicates that the emoji tokens do help with with picking the correct response, but don’t really aide when selecting alternative suitable candidates.

 One possible explanation is that when emoji are used (this is the case for 8.75% of all utterances), including those tokens helps matching those based on those emoji and their meaning. When there are no emoji present, our model might be just as clueless as the baseline.

77a2d4d2-2fad-4d5f-8b3f-9b44723436ad.label: text_chunk
77a2d4d2-2fad-4d5f-8b3f-9b44723436ad.text:
6 Conclusion and future work

In this paper we discussed the current state of emoji usage for conversational systems, which mainly lacks large baseline datasets. When looking at public datasets, conver- sational AI makers have to choose between dataset size and emoji support, with some datasets at least containing a few textual emoticons. We argued that this duality results in systems that fail to capture some information encoded in those emoji and in turn fail to respond adequately.

3b0a179b-a650-4ba4-8fc4-37efb54b1609.label: text_chunk
3b0a179b-a650-4ba4-8fc4-37efb54b1609.text: Based on this premise, we investigated how a response selection system based on BERT can be modified to support emoji. We proposed a format and tokenization method that’s indifferent to current Unicode specifications, and thus also works for datasets containing custom emoji.Evaluation of this emoji-aware system increased the 1-of-100 accuracy from 12.7% for the baseline to 17.8%.

0194fc4f-7fbe-45d6-a6e0-33837ac3724c.label: text_chunk
0194fc4f-7fbe-45d6-a6e0-33837ac3724c.text: Thus showing that supporting emoji correctly can help increasing performance for more casual systems, without having to rely on labeling or external descriptions for those emoji.However, the lack of high-quality, general datasets with emoji limits our conversa- tional model. Working towards larger casual conversational datasets would help both for our model, and for the conversational NLP community in general.7

01a619bc-c73c-4a9f-b5ce-66acdcc50365.label: text_chunk
01a619bc-c73c-4a9f-b5ce-66acdcc50365.text: We investigated the impact of emoji for conversational models and one could argue that these conclusions—or even the BERT model—can be generalized. We didn’t in- vestigate whether other tasks also benefited from our fine-tuned BERT model with the additional emoji tokens.During evaluation, we also observed utterances with only emoji characters. Even with our model that supports emoji, it could still be difficult to extract information like the subject of a conversation.

268843ad-ce28-4a26-a7e9-00a2838efb98.label: text_chunk
268843ad-ce28-4a26-a7e9-00a2838efb98.text:
Some of these utterances—but not all—were part of a larger conversation, so an interesting question could be how additional information affects the model.

Overall, the NTM serves as a sophisticated mechanism for memory management and contextual awareness in dialogue systems, while also influencing other neural architectures within its operational framework.
